design.title: Sigma-Delta ADC Indexed Design Reference
design.headline: Reusable Sigma-Delta ADC architecture, verification contract, and results wrapper.
design.summary: Indexed reference for a Sigma-Delta ADC top-level design so imported oversampled converter netlists can participate in the same architecture and presentation flow as other AMS designs.
design.narrative: This manifest captures the reusable architecture intent for a Sigma-Delta ADC reference design. It records the modulator partition, the core equations behind oversampling and noise shaping, the transient-oriented validation flow, and the file locations needed to regenerate both the design-reference page and the portfolio presentation.
design.technology: generic180 baseline with portability to generic130, generic65, and bcd180 through TechMapper wrappers
design.signoff_boundary: Current validation is a reusable top-level snapshot on an imported reference design. Replace with explicit sub-block implementations and foundry signoff flows for silicon work.

flow_summary: The Sigma-Delta ADC uses the same indexed flow as the rest of the framework: capture architecture, define verification settings, save a normalized snapshot result, and publish HTML plus portfolio outputs.
analysis_summary: Primary validation is transient-oriented because the key behavior is oversampled bitstream generation, feedback DAC interaction, and filtered-output evolution over time.
script_summary: Use the snapshot script to normalize the top-level run and the portfolio generator to publish architecture and status across designs.

architecture.summary: Analog input is integrated against a one-bit feedback DAC, quantized into a bitstream, and then low-pass filtered or decimated to reconstruct the in-band signal. The indexed wrapper keeps that architecture reusable across future implementations and technologies.
architecture.power_tree_summary: 3.3V supply and 0V/2.5V references establish the first-order Sigma-Delta operating range.

domains.name: VDD
domains.drives: integrator, quantizer, DAC model, decimation path

domains.name: VIN
domains.voltage: analog stimulus centered in converter range

power_sequence: Establish the reference endpoints before enabling the modulator loop.
Start the sampling clock so the integrator and quantizer enter a stable noise-shaping regime.
Observe the bitstream and filtered output over a long enough time window to assess behavior.

control_path.title: Summing path
control_path.description: Analog input and feedback DAC output form the modulator error signal.

control_path.title: Noise-shaping path
control_path.description: The integrator accumulates low-frequency error while the one-bit quantizer emits the high-rate bitstream.

control_path.title: Reconstruction path
control_path.description: The filtered or decimated output estimates the in-band analog value.

indexed_flow.title: Capture oversampled converter architecture
indexed_flow.description: Freeze the summing, integrating, quantizing, feedback, and filtering roles before implementation details change.

indexed_flow.title: Import or generate implementation
indexed_flow.description: Wrap the chosen Sigma-Delta source file into the indexed flow so reporting and reuse stay stable.

indexed_flow.title: Run the top-level snapshot
indexed_flow.description: Execute the transient snapshot and save a normalized regression JSON for later presentation.

indexed_flow.title: Publish reusable reference
indexed_flow.description: Generate the standalone HTML page and include the converter in the overall portfolio presentation.

analysis_playbook.name: Top-level modulator snapshot
analysis_playbook.purpose: Capture analog input, bitstream activity, and filtered-output behavior over an oversampled window.
analysis_playbook.pass_criteria: Transient results include analog input, bitstream activity, at least one filtered or internal node, and sufficient sample depth.

analysis_playbook.name: Portfolio publication
analysis_playbook.purpose: Surface the converter in the cross-design architecture and results presentation.
analysis_playbook.pass_criteria: The design appears in JSON, Markdown, and PPT outputs with its latest status.
analysis_playbook.debug_focus: Manifest completeness and saved regression artifact naming.

corner_closure.summary: No PVT closure is claimed yet. This wrapper records the intended architecture and validation contract for future technology-specific Sigma-Delta work.

matrix.process: generic180 baseline today; future TT/FF/SS and foundry corners

closure_order: Confirm bitstream and filtered-output visibility before claiming in-band performance.
Add explicit integrator, quantizer, DAC, and decimator verification before PVT closure.
Promote the imported reference into a technology-specific implementation only after architecture is frozen.
corner_closure.pass_rule: Current pass means the imported top-level source executed with the required visibility for architecture review. It does not mean silicon signoff closure.
corner_closure.model_note: Technology portability is architecture-driven today. Replace the imported reference with block-level implementations and foundry models for real development.

script_index.name: Run top-level snapshot
script_index.purpose: Create a normalized regression JSON from spec.yaml verification settings.

script_index.name: Generate Sigma-Delta webpage
script_index.purpose: Render the indexed Sigma-Delta reference as a standalone HTML page.

script_index.name: Generate portfolio PPT
script_index.purpose: Include the Sigma-Delta ADC in the overall architecture and results presentation.

debug_playbook.title: If the bitstream is missing
debug_playbook.description: Check that the imported source still exposes the quantizer output node expected by the snapshot checks.

debug_playbook.title: If the time window is too short
debug_playbook.description: Increase the transient stop time so the oversampled loop and filtered output have enough time to develop.

future_design_reuse.title: Swap in a different Sigma-Delta implementation
future_design_reuse.description: Only the source path and verification settings need to change when retargeting the wrapper to another oversampled ADC design.

future_design_reuse.title: Retarget technology
future_design_reuse.description: Keep the indexed architecture and update the implementation source plus technology metadata for the next node.

blocks.name: Sigma-Delta ADC Top Level
blocks.role: Converts an analog input into a noise-shaped bitstream and filtered low-rate estimate using oversampling.
blocks.architecture: The imported top-level reference models a first-order integrator, one-bit quantizer, feedback DAC, and simple output filtering in one reusable netlist.

implementation_steps.title: Form the modulator error
implementation_steps.description: Subtract feedback DAC output from the analog input at the summing node.

implementation_steps.title: Integrate and quantize
implementation_steps.description: The integrator accumulates low-frequency error and the quantizer emits the one-bit bitstream.

implementation_steps.title: Reconstruct the signal
implementation_steps.description: Filter or decimate the bitstream to recover the in-band output estimate.

primary_specs.name: Modulator order
primary_specs.reason: Defines the noise-shaping slope and architecture complexity.

primary_specs.name: Oversampling ratio
primary_specs.reason: Sets the relationship between sampling rate and in-band noise shaping.

primary_specs.name: Signal bandwidth
primary_specs.reason: Defines the in-band region for the filtered output.

sizing_formulas.title: Oversampling ratio
sizing_formulas.explanation: Relates sample rate to target signal bandwidth.

sizing_formulas.title: First-order SNR trend
sizing_formulas.formula: SNR improves as oversampling pushes quantization noise out of band
sizing_formulas.explanation: The first-order loop trades time-domain bit density for improved in-band resolution after filtering.

dc_operating_points.target: Centered analog sinusoid within converter range
dc_operating_points.how_set: Input source inside the imported reference netlist

dc_operating_points.target: High-rate one-bit output with density proportional to input
dc_operating_points.how_set: Quantizer output from the integrator state

dc_operating_points.target: Smoothed estimate of the in-band input
dc_operating_points.how_set: Simple reconstruction filter in the imported reference
dc_operating_points.why: Readable low-rate output estimate for validation.

analysis_steps.name: Top-level modulator snapshot
analysis_steps.purpose: Observe input, feedback, bitstream, and filtered-output behavior over an oversampled window.
analysis_steps.pass_criteria: Input waveform, bitstream, and at least one internal or filtered node are present with enough time samples.

corner_focus.risk: Integrator gain, comparator threshold, and decimation timing will all move differently across technologies.
corner_focus.primary_knob: Technology-specific sub-block implementation and timing budgets

tuning_knobs.title: Sampling rate
tuning_knobs.description: Directly changes OSR and the amount of shaped out-of-band noise.

tuning_knobs.title: Loop filter constant
tuning_knobs.description: Sets the effective integrator strength and therefore the bitstream density response.

files.role: Top-level verification contract for the Sigma-Delta wrapper
